summary:
Child mortality rates are determined for children aged 0-5 years because this period encompasses major causes such as pneumonia, diarrhea, and neonatal infections. This age range aligns with global health definitions and reflects the highest burden of disease among young children. 

Note: Do not include any commentary or explanation about the summary itself; only present it verbatim.
